title: Agropyron laeve
taxonomy: Agropyron laeve is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Agropyron, which is a group of perennial grasses commonly found in the Northern Hemisphere. Agropyron laeve is also known as smooth wheatgrass or smooth meadow grass, and it is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The species is further classified as a type of bunchgrass, which is characterized by its growth habit and ability to form dense clumps. Within the family Poaceae, Agropyron laeve is also classified as a type of Triticeae, a subfamily of grasses that includes wheat, barley, and rye.
